mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in 60% of cases or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a contraction in the industrial production index reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced factory output, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during tightening periods or yields a positive Sharpe ratio when shorted over a 1-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index readings force markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or forward returns are positive in 70% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls surpass 148 million, robust employment growth sustains wage-driven consumption and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates negative forward returns in 65% of triggered months or loses predictive power when tested against a baseline mean-reversion model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal spending reveals underlying demand resilience; when both series trend upward together, equity markets price in sustained earnings growth over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative information coefficient over 3-year rolling windows or fails to outperform single-series models in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment, rising industrial production signals successful supply chain normalization without triggering further rate hikes, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60% of observations during disinflation periods or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.
